videos.transcript: mumbasa Kenya during my travels around East Africa I've primarily visited just Capital Cities however I've heard so many good things about mumbasa that I just had to check it out for myself so in today's video we're going to be checking out things to do such as beaches nighti my accommodations and more so that you'll have an idea of things to do in the city if you ever decide to visit so sit back and relax as we explore mumbasa Kenya so for those that don't know Kenya is located in East Africa with Somalia and the Indian Ocean to the East Ethiopia and South Sudan to the north Uganda to the west and Tanzania to the South mumbasa is a coastal city in the southeastern region of Kenya and has a population of 1.3 million people making it the second largest city in Kenya so in the US I've had many co-workers that were from mumbasa and one thing they've always mentioned was how nice the beaches were so I made sure to make this one of my first stops during this trip the most popular beach in mumbasa is Pirates public beach it's famous for its Golden Sands that stretch along the Indian Ocean offering stunning views of the clear blue water what's interesting about this beach is that that during the mornings and early afternoon The Tide Is way out to sea so you'll see people playing far out where the water is but each hour after 300 p.m. you can literally see the tide coming in closer and closer by the minute and it looks pretty cool on the beach you'll find local vendors selling delicious snacks you can also do things on the beach such as camel riding jet skiing fishing or just take a swim they we also have a bunch of restaurants along the coast that serve pretty good food Pirates Beach is definitely a must visit when in mumbasa especially if you want to relax and soak up some sun now as you've seen in my previous videos Nairobi Kenya has one of the best nlife scenes in all of Africa so you know I had to see how it goes down in mumbasa the first Club I went to was Club hypnotica here they played a mix of majority afro beats as well as local music from Kenya and Tanzania plus a little Emma piano everyone was extremely friendly and the club stayed open well past Sunrise another place I party was 001 Lounge this place was definitely more spacious than club hypnotica and as it's a lounge had more of a chill Vibe all the clubs and bars I visited in the city play the same same type of music and all the DJs were very good including here at 001 just like Club hypnotica 001 has really good drink prices and I definitely prefer the lounge Vibes over a club the last place I visited and probably my favorite was Tapia cello bar and restaurant since this place was a bar it was easier to meet and chat with people while still having a DJ mixing up some good music it kind of felt like your typical neighborhood bar but more lively as you can imagine mumbasa night life isn't anywhere as good as nairobi's as Nairobi is the capital city but I had a great time at all the spots I visited in mumbasa and met some cool people now if you want to see some nature while in the city a good place to visit is heler Park this breathtaking Sanctuary is filled with lust green and amazing Wildlife the Serene environment with its tall trees and peaceful Lakes is perfect for a relaxing walk or picnic they have activities such as giraffe feeding crocodile feeding hippo feeding as well as other animals you can view like snakes and turtles just to name a few what makes Hiller Park unique is its blend of Nature and conservation definitely a beautiful and a unique place to visit in mumbasa now another thing that for sure stood out to me was the food in mumbasa every restaurant I visited was good now the first restaurant I had to visit was the popular Fort hany restaurant to grab some lunch Fort hany restaurant is located in the area of the city called Oldtown and is located right on the seat so you're going to get some great views the meal price are pretty fair averaging around $7 per meal however they don't sell alcohol as this is a Muslim restaurant I ordered the beef fet which was very tasty definitely one of my favorite restaurants during this trip another restaurant that had some really good food was a restaurant called the oak this restaurant had a nice little setup and sered some pretty good mocktails the food was a little on the expensive side but it was worth it as they had the best coconut fish ever another restaurant I enjoyed was Chara here you can select your Meats directly from the butcher the restaurant is very familyfriendly so there's people of all ages everywhere but it's also a Big Chill Spot in the city so many people come here to drink and listen to music as they also have a live DJ the restaurant is also also right on the beach so you're always going to have a cool breeze coming in the last restaurant I'll highlight is the beautiful instan buul beach cave restaurant it definitely offers a dining experience like no other as the restaurant is inside a cave the food is delicious serving mouthwatering Turkish and Kenyan dishes if you don't want to eat indoors the restaurant also has an outdoor setup right on the beach [Music] I loved every single restaurant I visited during this trip and I can't wait to come back and try more while in mumbasa I also visited the fort Jesus Museum also located in Oldtown and the interest fee was only $10 us for non-residents built by the Portuguese in 1593 the fort was designed to protect mumbasa from Invaders it played roles in many of the country's historic conflicts and later became a prison then in 1960 it officially became a museum as you explore the museum you'll see ancient cannons old Maps as well as artifacts that tell the story of mumbasa Rich past what makes Fort Jesus unique is its blend of architecture history and culture offering a glimpse into the battles and trade routes that shape the region you can almost feel the history come alive making it a must visit for history lovers now as far as my accommodations I stay in an Airbnb apartment located in the neighborhood of niali and was within walking distance of a shopping center as well as some of the clubs and bars you saw throughout the video the apartment was in a gated community with a security guard it only cost me about $35 per day it came to equipped with Wi-Fi a washing machine TV two bedrooms a community swimming pool and much more definitely worth every penny I loved my time in mumbasa beach towns are definitely my thing as they're usually fun but also very relaxing everyone I met was extremely welcoming which made me feel right at home the city kind of reminds me of a mini D Salon to be honest which is another city I love now just like I mentioned in my most recent Nairobi video US Passport holders do not need a visa to enter the country however you do need an electronic travel authorization which you can apply for online the online application is very easy to fill out and my travel authorization was approved in less than 24 hours if you're from mumbasa or have visited before let me know some things I can try out during my next trip because I 100% be coming back I'll see you in the next video have a good one

videos.title: Inside Mombasa City 2025 – Day & Night Transformation Tour
videos.description:
Mombasa City in 2025! Join me on an exclusive day & night tour as I show you how this historic coastal city is transforming. From iconic landmarks like the Mombasa Tusks and Mama Ngina Waterfront to vibrant street life, authentic Swahili food at Manga Boys, and the city’s buzzling nightlife—this is the real Mombasa you need to see.

👉 Perfect for travellers planning a trip and diaspora who want to reconnect with home.

Don’t forget to like, share & subscribe for more city transformation tours!
videos.transcript: Welcome to Mombasa, Kenya's oldest and second largest city. A place where ancient history meets modern transformation. Known for its coastal charm, cultural diversity, and economic importance, Mombasa in 2025 is not just a tourist destination. It is a city on the move. Good morning, good afternoon, good evening from wherever you are watching. Welcome to yet another episode. I'm your host Dolphin. I'm off. This is Nali, one of the richest neighborhoods here in Mombasa. So, I'm going to the city center. I'm going to be doing a walk tour there just to keep you updated how the Mombasa city looks like in 2025. So, come along with me. Make sure you watch this video till the end. If you're here for the very first time, you haven't subscribed, I don't know what you're waiting. Smash that subscribe button below. Give the video a very big thumbs up and let's do this. Let's go. Mombasa is more than 1,000 years old with a history shaped by the trade between Africa, the Middle East, and Asia. This blend creates a Swahili culture you'll see everywhere. From the architecture of the old town to the flavors of the Swahili food, this is a city that still proudly carries its past while embracing a modern future. [Music] Today Mombasa is undergoing a massive transformation. Roads are being expanded. Modern interchanges are being built. For instance, this is the Mombasa Malindi Highway under construction. Transport systems are also being upgraded to ease movement in and out of the city. Mombasa is quickly becoming a modern hub ready to serve both the locals and international trade. Real estate is also booming with new apartments, shopping malls and hotels redefining the city's skyline. Bakistan. >> Oh, Bakan Pakistan point. [Music] [Music] But beyond the big projects, it's the daily life here that truly defines Mombasa. The streets are alive with tuk tuks, motorbikes, and matos printed with vibrant art. Locals move at relaxed pace enjoying soil delicacies, fresh coconut water, and of course, business never stops at the city markets. For travelers, Mombasa offers an easy mix of work and play. Sunny beaches, cultural attractions, and a lifestyle that makes you feel right at home. [Music] [Music] I really love the views, guys. Mombasa really looks good, especially the blue and white buildings. [Music] [Applause] [Music] Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. [Music] We are already here at the famous Mamangina waterfront. A place where history, culture, and modern leisure come together. Whether you are a local or a visitor, this spot has something for everyone. It's a heritage site that overlooks the busy Kilindini harour, a reminder of Mombasa's deep connection with the Indian Ocean. Today, Mamina waterfront is a vibrant space. You can come here. You stroll along the scenic walkways as the ocean breeze hit your face. Families enjoy the children's play area and shed sitting spots. It also has amphitheaters that host cultural shows and events. And of course, you can't miss the food from the via mishkaki and freshly roasted maze. This is a true taste of the Swahili coast. Whenever I'm in Mombasa, I always make a stop at Manga Boys. It's in Kingrami and it's one of the best spot to enjoy authentic Swahili food. Whether it's biryani, pilawu, or fresh seafood straight from the coast. If you really want to taste the true flavors of Mombasa, this is the place to be. This is what we ordered. Biryani. And guys, I'm telling you, the taste was amazing. Their customer service was amazing. And I will definitely recommend this place. [Music] Oh, nice. As the sun sets, Mombasa transforms again. The streets light up with a new kind of energy. Cafes, restaurants fill up, music fills the air, and the city takes a vibrant glow. From the buzzling nightife to the peaceful night walks, Mombasa shows its charm in every corner. Okay. [Music] Today we are doing a walk in the streets of Mombasa. At night, guys, the vibes here is on another level. As you can see, the streets are well lit. So, stick around. Make sure you watch till the end. If you're here for the very first time, subscribe, like, and share. Let's go. [Music] [Applause] [Music] 11. [Music] Heat. [Music] [Music] We daddy. Yeah. >> Yeah. So cool. This is the famous. This is where you catch your buses to different parts of the country, especially longd distance buses. You can see we have Mombasa Kitui, Mombasa Nairobi, Mombasa Uganda, Mombasa Malava. Everywhere from this point you see the big buses. [Music] Ah, that is the dream line. So, we make a turn here. So guys this is Mari in Mombasa. This is where you will get all buses to long distance. If you want to go to Nairobi, Campala, everywhere in Kenya, this is where you'll get the buses. development, [Music] [Music] how people live and everything. >> We expect much better and good environment. >> Okay. In 5 years to come. Thank you. As you can see, tuk tuks and motorbikes are the easiest means of transport in Mobasa. You can easily maneuver unlike matus with this traffic condition.
